title: cli-commands.json
description: Phase 7 Evidence output: Authoritative mapping of control → generated CLI command. Produced FIRST (before execution) by /evidence skill so reviewers can audit commands before they run. Every control from mapping-results.json.controls[] must appear either in commands[] or not_cli_validatable[]. Commands are stored as argv arrays (never shell strings) to eliminate shell-injection risk.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when cli-commands.json was generated

stack_id.description: CloudFormation stack ARN from Phase 5 deploy (06-test/deployed-resources.json)

commands.description: CLI commands to execute for runtime evidence collection

not_cli_validatable.description: Controls that cannot be validated via runtime CLI probes (template-time only, policy-only, etc.)

_metadata.description: Optional metadata for traceability (git commit, operator, etc.)

synthesis_source.description: Traceability: what control metadata drove this synthesis (e.g., 'parameters_controlled[0]=$.lambda/CreateFunction.KMSKeyArn')

command_argv.description: Argv array passed directly to subprocess.run with shell=False. NEVER serialized through a shell — eliminates injection risk from physical_id/role-arn/property values flowing in from upstream phases.

command_str.description: Humanized shlex.join(command_argv) of the argv. For human-readable display in reports only. Programmatic consumers MUST use command_argv.

expression.description: Predicate expression (JMESPath query, regex pattern, or 'exit_code == 0'). Empty string is rejected — every predicate must have a body the executor can evaluate.

expected_pass.description: Human-readable description of what a PASS verdict means

expected_fail.description: Human-readable description of what a FAIL verdict means

output_log.description: Relative path to raw stdout/stderr log file (e.g., 'cli-outputs/CTRL-ACC-PRO-010--lambda-get-function-configuration.log')

expected_account.description: 12-digit AWS account ID extracted from stack_id. Execute layer asserts response ARN matches this account before recording PASS — prevents cross-account contamination.

reason_code.description: Machine-readable category for why this control cannot be CLI-validated. Programmatic consumers (validators, dashboards) key off this enum; the 'reason' field is for humans.

reason.description: Free-text human explanation; must be ≥20 chars and SHOULD reference what metadata is missing or which Phase 4/5 evidence file substantiates the control.

supplemental_evidence.description: Relative path to IaC-time or deploy-time evidence file (e.g., 'iac-time-evidence/cfn-guard.log'). Required when reason_code is 'iac-time-only' or 'policy-only-no-describe-api'.
